title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Warm up with our authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) recipe! Easy to follow, rich in flavor, and perfect for a comforting meal. Get the best Mercimek Corbasi recipe here!

best Turkish Red Lentil Soup at home
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?
Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step Guide
Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Turkish Red Lentil Soup, a staple in Turkish cuisine.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved and comforting Turkish red lentil soup, known for its vibrant color and smooth, creamy texture. This nourishing soup is a staple in Turkish homes, often served as a starter or a light meal. Its simple yet rich flavors come from red lentils, a hint of tomato paste, and a final flourish of mint-infused chili butter.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the diced carrot and potato, and continue to cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Stir in the rinsed red lentils and tomato paste. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly to toast the lentils slightly and deepen the flavor of the tomato paste.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend for Smoothness
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender to blend the soup until completely smooth. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Season the Soup
instructions.text: Return the pot to low heat. Stir in the salt and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth to reach your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Prepare the Mint-Chili Butter (Nane Yagli Salca)
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Add the dried mint and red pepper flakes. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn the butter or spices.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a generous amount of the fragrant mint-chili butter. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing.

tips: For a truly authentic flavor, do not skip the mint-chili butter; it is the signature finish for Mercimek Corbasi.
Ensure your lentils are thoroughly rinsed to remove any dust or debris, which can affect the soup's clarity and taste.
If you prefer a thinner soup, gradually add more hot water or broth after blending until it reaches your desired consistency.
article:
In the heart of Turkish cuisine lies a warmth that transcends borders, a comfort food cherished by millions: Mercimek Corbasi. This vibrant, flavorful, and incredibly nutritious red lentil soup is a staple in Turkish homes, gracing tables from bustling Istanbul cafes to cozy village kitchens. More than just a soup, Mercimek Corbasi is an embrace in a bowl, offering sustenance, flavor, and a taste of Anatolian hospitality. Whether you're seeking a hearty starter, a light lunch, or a comforting meal on a chilly evening, learning to make authentic Mercimek Corbasi will undoubtedly enrich your culinary repertoire. Let's delve into the world of this beloved Turkish classic, from its humble ingredients to its soul-satisfying preparation.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-mek chor-ba-suh," is essentially a creamy, savory soup made primarily from red lentils. It stands out with its characteristic reddish-orange hue, often brightened further by a drizzle of chili-infused mint oil or butter (nane yağı) and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before serving. This isn't just any lentil soup; it's a culinary cornerstone of Turkey, enjoyed year-round but particularly comforting during colder months. Its popularity stems from its simplicity, affordability, and the incredible depth of flavor achieved with relatively few ingredients.

Historically, lentils have been a vital food source across the Middle East and Mediterranean, prized for their nutritional value. Mercimek Corbasi embodies this tradition, transforming simple red lentils into a dish that is both nourishing and deeply satisfying. It's often served as the first course in a multi-dish Turkish meal, setting a delightful tone with its earthy notes and tangy finish. For many, the aroma of a simmering pot of Turkish Red Lentil Soup evokes memories of home and family gatherings.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

One of the beauties of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its straightforward ingredient list. You won't need exotic spices or hard-to-find components; instead, it relies on the quality and freshness of everyday staples to create its distinctive taste.

Here are the essential ingredients you'll need for an authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup:
*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Choose good quality, split red lentils, as they cook quickly and break down easily to create that signature creamy texture.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Finely chopped onion and minced garlic provide a sweet and pungent base that deepens the soup's flavor.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** A crucial ingredient for color and umami. Turkish tomato paste is often more concentrated and flavorful, but any good quality paste will work.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** Essential for the traditional topping. Dried mint, especially when bloomed in hot oil or butter, releases an incredible fragrance and flavor that defines Mercimek Corbasi.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** For a gentle warmth and color in the topping. Adjust to your preferred spice level.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** Used for sautéing the aromatics and for making the vibrant topping.
*   **Water or Broth:** Vegetable or chicken broth can be used to cook the lentils. Using broth adds an extra layer of flavor.
*   **Lemon:** Absolutely indispensable for serving. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice brightens the soup and cuts through its richness.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season the soup to perfection.

## How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step Guide

Making this comforting soup is surprisingly simple, even for novice cooks. Follow these steps to create your own delicious Turkish Red Lentil Soup:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, preventing the soup from becoming too cloudy. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a generous drizzle of olive oil (or a knob of butter) over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste:** Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly. This step helps to deepen its flavor and color.
4.  **Combine & Simmer:** Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the aromatics and tomato paste. Pour in the water or broth, along with salt and black pepper to taste. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very soft and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend Until Smooth:** Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (be cautious when blending hot liquids, only fill halfway and vent the lid) and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.
6.  **Adjust Consistency & Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust the seasoning (salt, pepper) as needed. Keep the soup warm over very low heat.
7.  **Prepare the Topping (Nane Yağı):** In a small pan, melt a tablespoon or two of butter or olive oil over medium-low heat. Once melted, add the dried mint and red pepper flakes. Sauté for about 30 seconds to 1 minute, just until the mint becomes fragrant and the butter/oil turns a vibrant red, being careful not to burn the spices. Remove from heat immediately.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving generously with the prepared mint and chili oil/butter topping. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing.

## Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Achieving that perfect, comforting bowl of Mercimek Corbasi is easy with a few key tips:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils:** Don't skip this step! It's crucial for a clear, vibrant soup.
*   **Sauté Properly:** Allow the onions to soften fully and the tomato paste to cook for a minute or two. This builds a rich flavor base.
*   **Blend Thoroughly:** For that signature creamy texture, blend until there are no visible lentil pieces left. A smooth consistency is key.
*   **The Nane Yağı is Non-Negotiable:** The mint and chili butter/oil topping is what truly elevates Mercimek Corbasi from a simple lentil soup to an authentic Turkish experience. The fragrant dried mint is essential.
*   **Fresh Lemon is a Must:** Always serve with lemon wedges. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the table brightens the flavors and adds a vital tangy counterpoint to the earthy lentils.
*   **Adjust to Taste:** Don't be afraid to taste and adjust salt and pepper throughout the cooking process.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways:

*   **As a Starter:** Traditionally, it's served as the first course of almost any Turkish meal, from everyday dinners to special occasions.
*   **A Light Meal:** Paired with some crusty bread (like a warm pita or traditional Turkish simit), it makes for a wonderfully satisfying and light lunch or dinner on its own.
*   **With Bread:** Always serve with plenty of fresh, crusty bread for dipping and soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup.
*   **Alongside Salads:** A simple, fresh green salad or a traditional Turkish shepherd's salad (çoban salatası) complements the soup beautifully, adding freshness and crunch.
*   **Beverage Pairings:** A glass of water, traditional Turkish tea, or even Ayran (a savory yogurt drink) are excellent non-alcoholic accompaniments.

## Variations and Dietary Adaptations

One of the great things about Mercimek Corbasi is its inherent adaptability and how well it caters to different dietary needs:

*   **Vegan/Vegetarian:** This soup is naturally vegan and vegetarian if you use vegetable broth and olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and the topping.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Red lentils are naturally gluten-free, making Mercimek Corbasi an excellent choice for those with gluten sensitivities. Just ensure your broth and tomato paste are also gluten-free.
*   **Adding Vegetables:** While traditional Mercimek Corbasi is quite simple, some variations include finely diced carrots or potatoes added with the lentils for extra nutrients and a slightly different texture.
*   **Spicier Kick:** If you love heat, feel free to increase the amount of red pepper flakes in the topping, or even add a pinch directly to the soup while it simmers.
*   **Herb Alternatives:** While dried mint is traditional for the topping, a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or cilantro can be used as a garnish for a different fresh note, though it will alter the authentic flavor profile slightly.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a recipe; it's an invitation to experience the heart of Turkish comfort food. Its simplicity, combined with its deeply satisfying flavors, makes it a timeless classic. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to savor a bowl of this truly magnificent Turkish Red Lentil Soup that will warm you from the inside out. Enjoy the journey into the vibrant world of Turkish cuisine!
